children.text: Any person that is determined, in accordance with paragraph (2), to have violated

children.tail: shall be required by order to pay a civil penalty in an amount not to exceed $25,000 for each violation. For the purposes of this paragraph, each day during which a violation of
continues shall constitute a separate violation of that section.

children.text: Before imposing a penalty against a person under paragraph (1), the head of an executive agency designated under

children.tail: shall provide the person with notice of the order. If, within 15 days after receiving the notice, the person requests a hearing, the head of the designated executive agency shall initiate a hearing on the violation.

children.text: Any hearing so requested shall be conducted before an administrative judge. The hearing shall be conducted in accordance with the requirements of

children.tail: . If no hearing is so requested, the order imposed by the head of the designated agency shall constitute a final agency action.

children.text: If the administrative judge determines, upon the preponderance of the evidence received, that a person named in the complaint has violated

children.tail: , the administrative judge shall state the findings of fact and conclusions of law, and issue and serve on such person an order described in paragraph (1).

children.text: Factors for determination of penalty amounts

In determining the amount of any civil penalty, the administrative judge or the head of the designated agency shall take into account the nature, circumstances, extent, and gravity of the violation or violations and, with respect to the violator, the ability to pay, effect on ability to continue to do business, any history of such violations, the degree of culpability, the existence of an internal compliance program, and such other matters as justice may require.
For the purposes of this paragraph, notice shall be in writing and shall be verifiably served upon the person or persons subject to an order described in paragraph (1). In addition, the notice shall—
set forth the time, date, and specific nature of the alleged violation or violations; and
specify the administrative and judicial remedies available to the person or persons subject to the order, including the availability of a hearing and subsequent appeal.

The decision and order of an administrative judge shall be the recommended decision and order and shall be referred to the head of the designated executive agency for final decision and order. If, within 60 days, the head of the designated executive agency does not modify or vacate the decision and order, it shall become a final agency action under this subsection.

A person adversely affected by a final order may, within 30 days after the date the final order is issued, file a petition in the Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit or in the Court of Appeals for the district in which the violation occurred.
If a person fails to comply with a final order issued against such person under this subsection and—
the person has not filed a petition for judicial review of the order in accordance with paragraph (4), or
a court in an action brought under paragraph (4) has entered a final judgment in favor of the designated executive agency,
the head of the designated executive agency shall commence a civil action to seek compliance with the final order in any appropriate district court of the United States.
In any such civil action, the validity and appropriateness of the final order shall not be subject to review.

Payment of penalties assessed in a final order under this section shall include interest at currently prevailing rates calculated from the date of expiration of the 60-day period referred to in paragraph (3) or the date of such final order, as the case may be.

children.tail: may, in addition to or in lieu of any civil penalty which may be imposed under subsection (a) for such violation, be fined under title 18, imprisoned for not more than five years, or both.
